mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month change in retail sales indicates near-term consumption momentum and improving revenue expectations, causing higher S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns no more often than the unconditional base rate over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals an adverse labor-income shock that lowers expected household spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE increases are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of out-of-sample cases.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid decline in the federal funds rate while unemployment is rising identifies emergency easing driven by recession risk, so earnings downgrades dominate discount-rate relief and S&P 500 returns are negative over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS falls by at least 0.5 percentage points over 3 months and UNRATE rises over the same 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint trigger has a nonnegative mean 20-day S&P 500 forward return or if the interaction term adds no predictive power versus the two single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month contraction in industrial production signals falling operating leverage and weaker goods-sector earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive INDPRO declines are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in housing starts indicates improving construction demand, household formation, and credit-sensitive activity, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises by at least 10% from its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST strength has a negative average 60-day S&P 500 forward return after transaction-date alignment and publication lags.

mechanism_narrative: Lower inflation together with a falling policy rate reduces expected discount rates without requiring tighter real financing conditions, raising equity valuations and S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth declines and FEDFUNDS falls over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint disinflation-and-easing trigger does not produce higher 20-day S&P 500 returns than periods with falling FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll decline indicates an abrupt hit to labor income and business confidence, leading investors to mark down near-term earnings and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change crosses below zero
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of publication-lag-adjusted triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales fall while unemployment rises, the combination identifies a demand shock with direct pressure on revenues and household income, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines over 3 months and UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS decline and UNRATE rise trigger has a nonnegative mean 20-day S&P 500 return or fails to outperform either proxy alone in directional accuracy.
